title: MR Angiography (time-of-flight / phase-contrast)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical medical-imaging literature for this principle.

E.description: MR Angiography (time-of-flight / phase-contrast): mri fourier encoding produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.rf_excitation -> L.tof_saturation -> L.kspace_readout -> int.spatial, with spatially-projected accumulation and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 3D vessel map. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~13; calibration-level mismatch (inplane_saturation, motion, turbulence_dephasing) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: TOF: unsaturated inflow enhances vessels vs saturated static tissue; PC: phi = gamma * venc * v
E.world_state_x: Patient-side quantity of interest (attenuation / activity / perfusion / stiffness etc.)
E.observation_y: Clinical sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D vessel map is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 13); inplane_saturation dominates the stability cliff; motion and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: analytic / closed-form + post-processing [MIP] | linear-operator + convex optimisation [Kalman-Vessel] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [MRA-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |inplane_saturation| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
